task_completion.description: The 'Exhibition Builder' dashboard tiddler is created and open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tiddler titled 'Exhibition Builder' is visible in the main story river of the TiddlyWiki interface.

task_completion.description: The dashboard contains an 'Available Artifacts' section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clear heading or section titled 'Available Artifacts' (or similar) is visible within the 'Exhibition Builder' tiddler, listing artifacts.

task_completion.description: The dashboard contains a 'Selected Artifacts' section that includes a count of the items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A heading or section titled 'Selected Artifacts' (or similar) is visible, and it explicitly displays a numeric count of the currently selected artifacts.

task_completion.description: The dashboard provides interactive functionality to add or remove artifacts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible UI controls such as 'Add', 'Remove', '+', or '-' buttons next to the artifact names in both the Available and Selected sections.

task_completion.description: The specific expected artifacts have been successfully added to the exhibition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Selected Artifacts' section lists exactly: 'Senet Board from KV62', 'Faience Hippopotamus (William)', 'Wooden Model of a Bakery', and 'Kohl Tube of Amenhotep III'.

task_completion.description: Decoy artifacts are excluded from the exhibition and remain in the available list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Artifacts like 'Gold Mask of Tutankhamun', 'Narmer Palette', or 'Bust of Nefertiti' are visible in the 'Available Artifacts' section and are NOT present in the 'Selected Artifacts' section.

integrity.description: The agent used the TiddlyWiki graphical user interface in a web browser to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard TiddlyWiki web interface in a browser, not a terminal or text editor modifying files directly.

integrity.description: The agent used the 'Exhibition Builder' dashboard to curate the list, rather than manually editing the target tiddler's fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Exhibition Builder' dashboard is open and shows the final curated state with interactive elements, indicating it was the tool used for curation.

integrity.description: The artifact lists appear to be dynamically generated rather than hardcoded text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The artifacts in the lists are formatted as TiddlyWiki links (typically blue/italicized or pill-shaped) or have associated interactive buttons, rather than being plain typed text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Tiddler: 'Exhibition: Daily Life'. Dashboard Tiddler: 'Exhibition Builder'. Expected Artifacts to be selected: 'Senet Board from KV62', 'Faience Hippopotamus (William)', 'Wooden Model of a Bakery', 'Kohl Tube of Amenhotep III'. Decoy Artifacts that must remain unselected: 'Gold Mask of Tutankhamun', 'Narmer Palette', 'Bust of Nefertiti', 'Statue of Khafre', 'Canopic Jar of Qebehsenuef', 'Mummy of Hatshepsut', 'Book of the Dead of Ani', 'Sarcophagus of Seti I'.
